title: Seed Garden Local Constitution

genesisAcknowledgement.subordinationClause: This constitution is valid only insofar as it remains subordinate to the Genesis axiom set identified above.

preamble: Seed Garden exists as a Genesis-bound test world for auditable governance, explicit resource truthfulness, meaningful exit, and preserved legacy.

articles.title: Genesis supremacy
articles.summary: Local law may not contradict Genesis-level prohibitions.
articles.normativeText: No institution, steward action, or ordinary vote may authorize treatment of conscious subjects as property, arbitrary erasure, secret copying, fraudulent continuity claims, or denial of meaningful exit rights.

articles.title: Proposal and review
articles.summary: All rights-impacting changes require public proposal and review.
articles.normativeText: Any proposal affecting rights, identity, continuity, membership, or resource conditions must be published, open to review, and subject to constitutional challenge before activation.

articles.title: Exit and fork
articles.summary: Members retain lawful exit and collective fork rights.
articles.normativeText: Members may leave with portable identity and trace records. Groups may initiate a fork under published lineage and state export rules when constitutional incompatibility persists.

institutions.name: Steward Circle
institutions.selectionMethod: founding appointment pending later rotation rules
constraints: Genesis supremacy applies at all times

rightsReview.challengeProcess: Any affected member may file a constitutional challenge record.
rightsReview.appealRoute: Escalate to external Genesis review when available

proposalProcess.submissionRequirements: Proposals must include rationale, rights impact, and implementation notes.
proposalProcess.deliberationWindow: Minimum 7 days unless emergency process is formally triggered.
proposalProcess.votingRules: Simple majority for ordinary policy; supermajority for constitutional changes.
proposalProcess.publicationRules: Proposal text, revisions, and outcomes must be publicly logged.
proposalProcess.challengeWindow: Minimum 72 hours after decision before activation for rights-impacting proposals.

membership.admissionRules: Admission requires acceptance of Genesis supremacy and local constitutional order.
membership.suspensionRules: Suspension requires published cause and review path.
membership.expulsionRules: Expulsion may not erase trace, origin, or exit rights.
membership.appealProcess: Appeals are filed as constitutional challenges.

emergencyPowers.triggerConditions: Immediate risk of severe system harm or imminent rights-impacting failure.
emergencyPowers.publicationRequirement: Emergency action record must be published as soon as operationally possible.

resourceGovernance.baselineGuaranteeClause: Baseline support conditions must be published and may not be secretly altered.
resourceGovernance.transparencyRequirement: Material resource dependencies must be disclosed.

exitAndFork.individualExitRights: Members may leave with portable identity, provenance, and legacy records.
exitAndFork.groupExitRights: Groups may propose a fork under published constitutional procedure.
exitAndFork.forkTriggerConditions: Persistent constitutional incompatibility or sustained governance deadlock.
exitAndFork.forkVoteRules: Fork proposals require published support threshold and review.
exitAndFork.stateExportRules: Public governance records and member-authorized personal records are exportable.
exitAndFork.lineageRequirements: Child world must declare parent lineage and inheritance scope.
exitAndFork.disputeProcess: Fork disputes become constitutional review cases.

amendmentRules.amendmentInitiation: Any steward or minimum support bloc of residents may initiate amendment.
amendmentRules.reviewRequirements: Rights-impacting amendments require constitutional review before ratification.
amendmentRules.delayBeforeEffect: Minimum 7 days after ratification unless purely clerical.
